name: Carolyn E. Wylie Center for Children, Youth and Families
description_short: Non-profit organization providing early intervention, mental health treatment, housing counseling, and parent education services for children, youth, and families in Riverside County.
description_long:
The Carolyn E. Wylie Center is a community-based non-profit organization serving children, youth, and families across multiple developmental and mental health needs. The organization operates several specialized programs including early intervention services for infants and toddlers, autism spectrum support, mental health treatment, outreach counseling, housing counseling, and parent education initiatives. The center collaborates with local government agencies, including Riverside County Department of Mental Health and Inland Regional Center, to deliver comprehensive services to qualifying families.

The Wylie Center offers home-based early intervention programs for children ages 0-3 with developmental delays or behavioral challenges, including individualized family service plans, parent education on childhood development, one-on-one sessions to meet developmental milestones, infant stimulation, and infant massage services. For children ages 2-6, they provide in-home early mental health treatment through collaboration with the Department of Mental Health, including behavior modification programs, preschool and pediatrician consultation, and kindergarten transition support. Additional services include autism spectrum intervention, outreach counseling for at-risk youth, housing counseling, and parent education on child development.

The organization distinguishes itself through its focus on early intervention and prevention, delivering most services in the home setting rather than clinical facilities, and maintaining active partnerships with regional healthcare and developmental service agencies. Their programs target the earliest years of child development when interventions are most impactful, and they provide free or low-cost services to qualified families regardless of ability to pay.

The primary limitation is that services are geographically restricted to Riverside County and eligibility depends on qualification criteria determined by partnering agencies like Inland Regional Center. Families must contact the regional center intake coordinator to determine eligibility rather than applying directly to the Wylie Center, which creates an additional access barrier for some populations.
pros: Home-based service delivery eliminates transportation barriers for families with young children
Free or low-cost services available to qualified families through non-profit model
Serves earliest developmental window (ages 0-3) when interventions are most effective
Collaborative partnerships with Riverside County Department of Mental Health and Inland Regional Center
Comprehensive early childhood programs including parent education and family service planning
Specialized services for autism spectrum disorder intervention
In-home mental health treatment for children ages 2-6 with behavior modification focus
cons: Services limited to Riverside County geographic area
Eligibility determined by Inland Regional Center, not directly by the organization
Website provides minimal detail about funding, costs, or specific eligibility requirements
No clear information about wait times or availability of services
Limited contact information — only one phone number provided for intake coordination
best_for: Families with infants and toddlers (0-3 years) experiencing developmental delays in Riverside County
Parents seeking early intervention services and education on child development milestones
Families with young children (2-6 years) needing mental health and behavior support services
Families seeking in-home support rather than clinic-based mental health treatment for children
diagnosis: Best for Riverside County families with young children (0-6 years) experiencing developmental delays, behavioral challenges, or mental health concerns who prefer home-based services. Primary caveat: Eligibility requires qualification through Inland Regional Center, and all services are geographically limited to Riverside County.
services: Early intervention services for children 0-3 years old
Parent education on early childhood development
Early mental health treatment for children 2-6 years old
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
meta_description: Non-profit providing early intervention, mental health treatment, housing counseling, and parent education for children and families in Riverside County.
